---
title: "day3_reproducibility_notes"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Conda Environment Setup

## Step 1: Searching for FastQC

```{bash}
#| eval: false
conda search -c bioconda fastqc
```

This particular command will provide us with all the version available for that particular package in that specific channel.

## Step 2: Creating environment

```{bash}
#| eval: false
conda create --name week1 -y  # -y means "yes" to all questions automatically
```

This command will create a env named week1

## Step 3: Activating the environment

```{bash}
#| eval: false
conda activate week1
```

this step will helps to activate the virtual environment week1

## Step 4: Installing packages in the environment activated in step 3

```{bash}
#| eval: false
conda install -c bioconda fastqc=0.11.8 -y
```

This will install the package fastqc with the particular version through a specific channel bioconda

```{r}
library(fastqcr)
```

# Docker environment setup

## Step 1: Pulling an image

```{bash}
#| eval: false
docker pull --platform linux/amd64 <image_name>
```

```{bash}
#| eval: false
# Pull the BUSCO image (a common genomics tool)
# Format: docker pull <author>/<tool>:<version>
docker pull ezlabgva/busco:v6.0.0_cv1
```

## Step 2: Running a Docker

```{bash}
#| eval: false
# Breakdown of the command:
# --rm  : Automatically delete the container when the task is finished (saves space)
# -it   : Interactive Terminal (lets us see the output in real-time)
# -v    : Volume mapping. This connects your computer's folder to the container's folder.
#         Without this, the container cannot see your data!

docker run --rm -it ezlabgva/busco:v6.0.0_cv1 busco --help
```
